metadata.name: ISO 14064 Greenhouse Gas Accounting and Verification
metadata.description: ISO 14064 is a three-part international standard for greenhouse gas (GHG) accounting and verification. Part 1 covers organizational-level GHG inventories, Part 2 covers project-level GHG reductions, and Part 3 covers validation and verification of GHG assertions. It provides the technical foundation for carbon accounting and reporting.
metadata.classification_reasoning: ISO 14064 is the international standard for GHG accounting and verification, providing the technical framework for carbon inventories and third-party assurance used in corporate sustainability reporting.
metadata.framework_purpose: To provide guidance for quantification, monitoring, reporting, and verification of greenhouse gas emissions and removals at the organizational and project levels.
metadata.framework_scope: Organizations of all types and sizes seeking to quantify, monitor, and report GHG emissions and removals at the organizational level, or verify GHG claims and inventories.
framework_objectives: Establish a credible and consistent GHG inventory
Enable comparison of GHG performance over time
Support carbon markets and offsetting programs
Enable third-party verification of GHG claims
Align with global GHG reporting requirements (TCFD, CSRD, SEC)
metadata.target_audience: Sustainability Officers, Environmental Managers, Carbon Accountants, GHG Verifiers, CFOs, and organizations with GHG reporting obligations
adoption_approach: Identify and quantify GHG sources and sinks
Select base year and establish baseline
Implement GHG data management and monitoring
Report GHG inventory in accordance with standard

controls.title: Organizational Boundaries
controls.description: Define and document the organizational boundaries for the GHG inventory using either equity share, financial control, or operational control approach.
controls.full_text: Define the organizational boundary for GHG accounting using one of three consolidation approaches: (1) Equity share — account for GHG proportional to ownership, (2) Financial control — account for GHG from operations over which financial control is held, (3) Operational control — account for GHG from operations for which the organization has operational control. Document the approach selected and apply consistently.

controls.title: Operational Boundaries and GHG Source Identification
controls.description: Identify and categorize all GHG sources, sinks, and reservoirs within the operational boundary including Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions.
controls.full_text: Identify and document all GHG emission sources (Scope 1 direct, Scope 2 indirect from purchased energy, and Scope 3 other indirect). Classify sources into direct emissions (Scope 1), energy indirect (Scope 2), and other indirect (Scope 3). Assess materiality of each source for inclusion or exclusion. Justify any excluded sources.

controls.title: GHG Quantification
controls.description: Quantify GHG emissions and removals using appropriate methods, emission factors, and activity data.
controls.full_text: Quantify all identified GHG emission sources and sinks using appropriate methods: direct measurement, mass balance, or emission factor approaches. Apply recognized emission factors (IPCC, DEFRA, EPA, IEA). Convert all GHGs to CO2 equivalent using Global Warming Potential (GWP) values. Document calculation methodology for each source.

controls.title: GHG Base Year
controls.description: Select and document a GHG base year, establish the base year inventory, and define recalculation policy.
controls.full_text: Select a base year that is representative of typical emissions and has reliable data. Establish the base year inventory for each quantified source. Define a recalculation policy specifying conditions under which the base year will be recalculated (significant acquisitions, divestitures, methodology changes). Apply consistent methodology across reporting years.

controls.title: GHG Inventory Documentation and Reporting
controls.description: Document and report the GHG inventory in a manner that is accurate, complete, consistent, transparent, and relevant.
controls.full_text: Prepare a GHG inventory report or statement including: total Scope 1 and 2 emissions (and Scope 3 where applicable), base year emissions and changes, methodology description, organizational and operational boundary description, emission factors and GWP values, data quality assessment, and uncertainty analysis. Apply the five GHG reporting principles: accuracy, completeness, consistency, transparency, and relevance.

controls.title: GHG Data Quality Management
controls.description: Implement data management systems and quality controls to ensure GHG data is accurate, complete, and reliable.
controls.full_text: Implement GHG data management systems including: data collection procedures, quality control checks, data entry validation, uncertainty analysis, gap-filling methodology for missing data, and data governance. Maintain audit trails for all GHG data. Implement internal review processes before finalizing the inventory.

controls.title: Internal GHG Inventory Review
controls.description: Conduct internal review of the GHG inventory before seeking external verification.
controls.full_text: Implement internal review processes to validate GHG data accuracy, completeness, and methodology consistency before external verification. Internal reviewers should be independent of the data collection team. Review should cover: data accuracy, completeness, consistency, boundary compliance, and documentation quality.

controls.title: GHG Verification Planning
controls.description: Plan the GHG verification engagement including scope, materiality threshold, and level of assurance.
controls.full_text: Plan the GHG verification engagement including: verification scope (which years, which scopes), materiality threshold (typically 5% for reasonable assurance, higher for limited assurance), level of assurance (reasonable or limited), verification criteria (ISO 14064-1, GHG Protocol), and verification team qualifications.

controls.title: GHG Verification Evidence Collection
controls.description: Collect sufficient and appropriate evidence to support the verification conclusion.
controls.full_text: Collect verification evidence through: document review (policies, procedures, calculations), data testing (sampling activity data, checking emission factors), site visits (if required), and management inquiry. Evidence must be sufficient and appropriate to support the verification conclusion at the stated assurance level.

controls.title: GHG Verification Statement
controls.description: Issue a verification statement expressing an opinion on whether the GHG assertion is free from material misstatement.
controls.full_text: Issue a GHG verification statement that includes: the verifier's identity, the scope of verification, the criteria applied, the level of assurance provided, any material misstatements identified, and the verification conclusion. For reasonable assurance: 'the GHG inventory presents fairly.' For limited assurance: 'nothing has come to our attention.'

controls.title: GHG Intensity and Performance Tracking
controls.description: Track and report GHG emissions intensity metrics to measure progress in decoupling emissions from business growth.
controls.full_text: Define and track GHG emissions intensity metrics relevant to the organization (e.g., tCO2e per unit of revenue, per unit of production, per employee). Monitor intensity trends relative to base year. Report intensity improvements that demonstrate decoupling of emissions from growth. Use intensity metrics to set and track science-based targets.

controls.title: Scope 3 Value Chain Emissions
controls.description: Account for and report Scope 3 upstream and downstream emissions across the value chain using GHG Protocol Scope 3 standard.
controls.full_text: Identify and quantify material Scope 3 emission categories using the GHG Protocol Corporate Value Chain (Scope 3) Standard. Categories include: purchased goods and services, capital goods, fuel and energy activities, upstream transportation, waste, business travel, employee commuting, investments, use of sold products, and end-of-life treatment. Engage suppliers to improve Scope 3 data quality.

controls.title: Carbon Credit and Offset Management
controls.description: Manage voluntary carbon credits and offsets used to address residual emissions in net zero strategies.
controls.full_text: Manage carbon credits and offsets using high-quality standards (Verra VCS, Gold Standard, ACR). Ensure offsets represent additional, permanent, verifiable, and exclusive emissions reductions. Retire offsets in recognized registries to prevent double counting. Disclose offset use separately from emission reductions. Apply Science Based Targets initiative removals guidance.
evidence_requirements: Separation of offsets from emission reductions in reporting

controls.title: GHG Reporting Regulatory Alignment
controls.description: Align the GHG inventory with applicable regulatory reporting requirements and voluntary frameworks.
controls.full_text: Align GHG inventory with applicable regulatory requirements (EU ETS, UK ETS, SEC climate disclosures, CSRD) and voluntary frameworks (TCFD, GRI 305, ISSB IFRS S2, SASB). Ensure the ISO 14064-1 inventory meets the data quality requirements for regulatory submissions. Coordinate with legal teams on regulatory disclosures.

controls.title: Science-Based GHG Reduction Targets
controls.description: Establish GHG reduction targets aligned with climate science and the Paris Agreement.
controls.full_text: Set science-based GHG reduction targets aligned with keeping global warming to 1.5°C. Submit targets to the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) for validation. Set near-term targets (5-10 years) and long-term net zero targets (by 2050). Report annual progress against targets. Implement action plans to achieve targets.
